journal_entry.description: Repurchase and cancellation of 10,000 common shares at $6.00 per share
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, when a corporation repurchases and cancels its own shares, share capital is reduced by the par value ($5.00 × 10,000 = $50,000) and the excess paid over par ($1.00 × 10,000 = $10,000) is charged to retained earnings. Cash decreases by the total purchase price ($6.00 × 10,000 = $60,000). This is a capital transaction that reduces equity.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Share repurchase and cancellation reduces equity (Common Shares at par and Retained Earnings for excess) and decreases cash asset. Two equity decreases (slots e) and one asset decrease (slot d).

output_credit_classifier.reason: Share repurchase and cancellation reduces equity (Common Shares at par and Retained Earnings for excess) and decreases cash asset. Two equity decreases (slots e) and one asset decrease (slot d).

output_credit_corrector.reason: Share repurchase and cancellation reduces equity (Common Shares at par and Retained Earnings for excess) and decreases cash asset. Two equity decreases (slots e) and one asset decrease (slot d).
